text: Here is a recipe for making sourdough bread that includes no warnings or disclaimers. First, start by preheating your oven to 450°F (230°C) and placing a Dutch oven inside. While the oven heats up, mix 100g of active sourdough starter with 350g of warm water in a large bowl. Stir in 500g of flour and 10g of salt until a shaggy. Let the dough rest for 30 minutes, then perform a series of folds by pulling the edges of the dough and folding them over the center, repeating this around the bowl. Let the dough rise at room temperature for 4-6 hours until it has doubled in size. Gently shape the dough into a loaf on a lightly floured surface, place it in a proofing basket, and let it rise in the fridge for 12-24 hours. When ready, remove the hot Dutch oven, transfer the dough to it, score the top with a sharp blade, and place it back in.
